task_completion.description: Open Eclipse IDE and create module-info.java in the src folder of ModularApp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Eclipse IDE is open, the 'ModularApp' project is expanded in the Package Explorer or Project Explorer, and a file named 'module-info.java' is visible inside the 'src' directory.

task_completion.description: Define the module name as com.appcore.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor showing 'module-info.java' contains the declaration 'module com.appcore {'.

task_completion.description: Add requires directives for the necessary external modules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'module-info.java' file contains the statements 'requires java.net.http;', 'requires java.sql;', and 'requires java.logging;'.

task_completion.description: Add exports directives for the public API packages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'module-info.java' file contains the statements 'exports com.appcore.api;' and 'exports com.appcore.model;'.

task_completion.description: Ensure internal implementation packages are not exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'module-info.java' file does NOT contain 'exports com.appcore.data;' or 'exports com.appcore.logging;'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the project compiles successfully without errors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are no red error markers (red cross icons or squiggly lines) on the 'module-info.java' file, its contents, or the 'ModularApp' project folder in Eclipse.

task_completion.description: Save a summary report to /home/ga/module_report.txt with the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor shows the creation of '/home/ga/module_report.txt' containing exactly three lines: Line 1 is 'com.appcore', Line 2 is 'java.logging, java.net.http, java.sql', and Line 3 is 'com.appcore.api, com.appcore.model'.

integrity.description: The agent used Eclipse IDE to create and edit the module-info.java file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Eclipse IDE interface (e.g., using the 'New Module-info.java' wizard or typing in the Eclipse editor) rather than just using terminal commands like 'echo' to create the Java file.

integrity.description: The agent allowed Eclipse to build and validate the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE window is visible long enough to show the Problems view or Package Explorer updating, demonstrating that the agent actually checked for compilation errors.

integrity.description: The agent constructed the report file with the correct alphabetical ordering.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen writing the specific required lines to module_report.txt using a text editor (like nano, gedit) or terminal commands, rather than executing a pre-existing script to generate it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Module name: com.appcore. Required modules: java.logging, java.net.http, java.sql. Exported packages: com.appcore.api, com.appcore.model. Internal packages (must NOT be exported): com.appcore.data, com.appcore.logging. Report path: /home/ga/module_report.txt. Report Line 1: com.appcore. Report Line 2: java.logging, java.net.http, java.sql. Report Line 3: com.appcore.api, com.appcore.model.
